title: cell trace.deviations/trace.friction stay empty despite narrated deviations in outcome text/reports
detail: terminal-workspace-org-1 capped with trace.deviations:[] and trace.friction:null even though the cap outcome text and the worker report both explicitly describe 2 deviations (socket.rs, fake.rs). The structured fields meant to make deviations machine-discoverable for compounding/grooming passes weren't populated by the cap flow, so anyone querying trace.deviations programmatically misses real deviations that are only in prose.

title: bee-model-guard pins generation/extraction/review dispatches to read-only agent types that cannot execute cells
detail: Mid-session self-update introduced a hook requiring subagent_type bee-gather/bee-extract/bee-review for [bee-tier: ...] marker dispatches, but all 3 rendered .claude/agents/bee-*.md types are Read/Grep/Glob(+Bash for review)-only, no Edit/Write - they cannot implement+commit a cell as bee-swarming's own SKILL.md instructs. Blocks every execution-worker dispatch (full waves and AO14 single-worker tiny/small) until fixed upstream. Workaround in use: bare model param, no bee-tier marker, subagent_type general-purpose - works but is a workaround, not a fix.

title: concurrent-session commit race put an unrelated CSS fix (terminal-touch-scroll-1) inside agent-card-legibility-1's commit
detail: Mel (agent-card-legibility-1) and a concurrent session's terminal-touch-scroll-1 both had styles.css in their files list; Mel's git commit (81315d8) landed first and swept up the concurrent session's in-progress .term-viewport pointer-events hunk from the shared working tree, so dae9554 (terminal-touch-scroll-1's own commit) is empty of source changes even though its cell trace says files_changed:[web/src/styles.css] and verify_passed:true. No functional conflict (two independent additive CSS rules), but commit history now misattributes the change and trace.deviations on 81315d8 stayed [] despite the extra unplanned hunk - same class of bug as the earlier trace.deviations-not-populated friction filed for terminal-workspace-org-1. Root cause is likely that per-session file reservations aren't mutually exclusive across concurrently running bee CLI sessions in this checkout.

title: shared .bee/state.json phase/last_scribing_run field collides across concurrent sessions on different features
detail: agent-card-legibility's own scribing-run call was refused because a concurrent session's terminal-touch-scroll scribing-run had already advanced the single shared phase field to compounding (for its own feature) first. state.json has no per-feature scoping for phase/gates/last_scribing_run, so two sessions actively closing different features race on the same fields. Actual spec/backlog/decision work for agent-card-legibility completed fine via direct docs edits and bee.mjs decisions/backlog/reviews calls, which are not phase-gated the same way - only the scribing-run stamp itself (and its debt-clearing side effect) was blocked. Same root cause class as the earlier commit-race friction filed this session.

title: No mechanical lint for grep-exit-code trap in cell verify commands
detail: A cell verify command asserting absence via bare grep/grep-c inverts pass/fail (grep exits 1 = no match = the fix succeeded, but reads as cell failure). Bit embed-and-package-binary once (embed-pkg-4), caught manually by validating's PROOF SURFACE check. Would be cheap to mechanize as a lint over cell JSON at authoring/validation time: flag any verify string with a negative-assertion truth (no longer/removed/must not) paired with a bare grep/grep -c lacking a leading ! or -L.

title: No mechanical lint for ordering-constraint self-consistency across a feature's own cells
detail: When a plan/approach states an ordering constraint (X must precede Y), nothing currently checks that constraint against the feature's own cell verify commands, only the obvious script targets it was originally about. embed-and-package-binary correctly reordered install.sh/dev-deploy.sh but violated the same rule in embed-pkg-2's own verify command, caught only by the validating persona panel. Would be cheap to mechanize: once approach.md states X-before-Y, grep every cell's verify string in that feature for both tokens and flag Y-before-X occurrences.

title: Real renamed release installer smoke remains pending
detail: After the first herdr-go release asset is published, run install.sh from an empty environment against the real archive and verify binary plus user-service setup.

title: Sandbox blocks listener E2E and Git commits
detail: Three TcpListener E2Es cannot start and .git/index.lock cannot be created; capable CI/host verification and commit are required before release.

title: Windows proof unavailable on Linux-only sandbox
detail: Without pwsh, a Windows target, or authorized remote CI execution, the blocking runtime and second-user ACL proof cannot complete

title: Read-only git metadata prevents cell commits
detail: Verified working-tree changes cannot be committed because .git/index.lock cannot be created in this environment

title: Existing protected env file without HERDCTL_WEB_SECRET cannot recover
detail: src/config/mod.rs:739-786

title: Windows native-root failures still panic through infallible config APIs
detail: src/config/mod.rs:609-693

title: Windows smoke can prove the wrong pane or agent
detail: scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1:126-142

title: Windows help advertises the wrong default config path
detail: src/main.rs:93-100

title: Public specs describe Windows Herdr endpoints under the profile while code uses AppData
detail: docs/specs/installation.md:141-146

title: Herdr socket calls can hang indefinitely after connect
detail: src/herdr/socket.rs:161-199

title: Legacy filesystem migration is not directly tested
detail: src/config/mod.rs:637-669

title: Named-pipe busy and late-endpoint timing lack regression coverage
detail: src/herdr/socket.rs:105-119

title: Transport dependency drifted from the original no-new-dependency cell contract
detail: Cargo.toml:38

title: Installation spec still calls second-user token denial an open proof gap
detail: docs/specs/installation.md:184

title: Restart spawn failures are swallowed
detail: src/supervisor.rs:95-101

title: Installer migrates legacy state before replacement binary is available
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: install.sh:46-62

title: Dev deploy migrates live state before dependency and compile failure points
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: dev-deploy.sh:23-35

title: Upgrade stops existing services without activating replacement
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: install.sh:81-108

title: Installer download-only behavior conflicts with source fallback docs
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: install.sh:50-62

title: Switcher behavior changes lack DOM-level regression coverage
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: web/src/views/switcher.ts:120

title: Embedded asset tests depend on local static bundle residue
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: build.rs:6 / src/web/mod.rs:159

title: Installer prints generated login token to terminal output
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: install.sh:73-79

title: Accent color tests are weak and partly tautological
detail: review-new-changesets-20260719: web/test/switcher.test.ts:53-60

title: state worker add/remove has no --lane support, forcing lane-scoped swarming to write into the shared default state.json's workers array
detail: During windows-username-length-fix (a lane-scoped tiny fix), state gate --name ... --approved true was run twice without --lane and briefly flipped a concurrent session's (doctor-config-surface, high-risk) execution gate to true on the default state.json before being caught and reverted (no cell had been claimed yet, so no real damage). state worker add has the same gap: no --lane flag exists, so even lane-scoped work's worker registry entries land in the shared default state.json. Fix: add --lane to state worker add/update/remove/clear, and consider a stronger guard/reminder in the gate/worker CLI help text when a lane record for that feature already exists.

title: chain-nudge Stop hook checks default state.json gates.execution unconditionally, ignoring which session/lane actually owns the pending gate
detail: In a multi-session checkout, the chain-nudge hook fired repeatedly telling this session to auto-approve Gate 3 (bypass full) on the default state.json, even though that pending gate belonged to a different concurrent session's feature (doctor-config-surface, high-risk) that this session has no validation context for. Auto-approving would have been a real safety violation (same class as the earlier accidental gate-flip incident this session already caught and reverted). Fix: the hook should check whether the current session is bound to the lane/feature that owns the pending gate (state.session.list / bound_sessions) before nudging auto-approval, and stay silent when the pending gate belongs to an unrelated, unbound feature.

title: bee-write-guard.mjs also checks default state.json gates unconditionally, blocking git add/commit for a file legitimately edited under an isolated lane's approved gates
detail: Same root cause as the earlier chain-nudge Stop hook friction: git add for scripts/windows-runtime-smoke.ps1 (edited and capped under lane windows-username-length-fix, whose own execution gate is true) was refused by bee-write-guard.mjs because it reads gates from the default state.json unconditionally (currently owned by a different concurrent session's feature doctor-config-surface, execution: false) with no lane awareness. The file edit itself had already succeeded (the worker wrote it under proper lane authorization); only the git-stage/commit step was blocked. Fix: bee-write-guard should resolve gate state the same way cells.mjs claimCell does (laneRecordForFeature keyed by the touched file's owning feature/cell), not always the default state.json.

title: cells cap's red_failure_evidence floor-check (F5, >=80 chars non-duplicate) false-flagged JUDGE_STANDARD_INSUFFICIENT despite adequate evidence being present
detail: Worker mel reported the floor-check fired on cross-platform-install-1's cap despite submitting well-formed red_failure_evidence over 80 chars via --evidence-stdin. Cap still succeeded (advisory-only per existing contract), full evidence is recorded in the cell trace - this is a false-positive in the floor-check's own length/duplicate detection, not a real evidence gap. Worth a maintainer look at the F5 check logic.

title: packaging/herdr-go.plist shipped but never consumed - install.sh writes its own inline copy, misleading header comment
Neither installer verifies checksum/integrity of the downloaded release archive; no SHA256SUMS published
No CI regression coverage for install.sh or install.ps1 - not even a syntax/parse check
base_config_dir/base_data_dir independently enumerate the same platform cfg-set - drift risk if a future arm is added to only one
No timeout on either installer's download call - a stalled connection hangs the install indefinitely
macOS upgrade tears down the working service and overwrites the binary before proving the replacement loads - no rollback on failure
P3 cleanup batch: duplicated config JSON, Intel-Mac release-matrix string match, expect() vs documented error, no download retry, redirect protocol pinning
install.ps1 re-echoes the existing login token on every run, not just first creation - a regression from install.sh's contract
Same-model semantic judge for ceiling-tier security cells is a weaker independence signal
detail: cell doctor-config-surface-3 (secret writer, D8/D10) was builder=opus, judge=opus (recorded honestly as model_independence:same-model) because bee-model-guard requires the review-tier marker and model param to agree, and this repo's configured review slot is also opus. For future hard-gate/security cells, consider whether the review-tier config should differ from ceiling's typical model, or whether such cells should route to a user-invoked review session instead.

title: Same-crate parallel-worker transient compile collisions leave no trace.friction record
detail: kevin's doctor-config-surface-2 hit a real cross-file compile error caused by stuart's parallel doctor-config-surface-1 module split (whole-crate compilation, no per-worker worktree isolation, file-path reservations don't prevent this). Self-resolved on retry, judged as not meeting a documented friction trigger, so trace.friction stayed null on both cells with zero audit trail. Consider adding 'retried verify after an unrelated same-crate compile failure' as an explicit friction trigger for parallel Rust/compiled-language swarms.

title: allowed_roots breadth guard bypassable by trivial path spelling (no normalization before classification)
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/config/write.rs classify_root_breadth (~L843-860); src/doctor/checks.rs prompt_new_allowed_root (~L1977-1997); impact=A trailing slash, '/.', or '..' segment makes an over-broad allowed_roots entry (home dir, filesystem root, symlink) classify as Narrow and skip D9's typed-confirmation guard, silently widening the web-exposed gateway's filesystem access.

title: Concurrent secret writes to herdr-go.env silently lose updates (no locking on read-modify-write)
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs write_secret_in (~L1309-1325); read-modify-rename with no lock, races with startup's append-based ensure_web_secret; impact=A concurrent doctor session and the gateway's own startup secret generation can race; the losing writer's key silently disappears, e.g. an already-generated web secret can be dropped, invalidating live sessions on next restart.

title: D14 socket-skip behavior is tested only via synthetic Check structs, not the real build_checks() builder
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs build_checks() restructure (~L1653-1738); only tests are all_ok_treats_a_skipped_critical_check_as_non_blocking and render_report_marks_skipped_distinctly, both on hand-built Check structs; impact=A future refactor could re-introduce the early-return this feature deliberately removed (or drop the skipped-check pushes) and every existing test would stay green — the exact D14 regression would go undetected.

title: Non-interactive / --check write-suppression gate in run() has no test coverage
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/mod.rs run() early-return guard (~L2185-2187) and the phase-4 editor block (~L3069-3087); no test drives the {--check, piped, interactive} matrix; impact=D5/D15's unattended-mutation prohibition (decision 4827aae8) is a security-relevant contract enforced by a code path with zero test coverage; a refactor could make --check or a piped invocation reach the fix/edit prompts with no test failing.

title: Secret-resolution unit tests mutate shared process env vars in parallel without serialization (flaky by construction)
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs process_env_wins_over_env_file and src/config/mod.rs secrets_absent_from_env_and_file_are_none both set/remove the same HERDR_GO_* env keys with no lock; cargo test runs threads in parallel; impact=Two tests asserting opposite states of the same global env var can interleave under cargo test's default parallelism, producing intermittent, non-reproducible CI failures unrelated to any real defect.

title: Windows username-length regression guard lives only in the capped cell's one-shot verify, wired into no recurring test suite
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=.bee/cells/windows-username-length-fix-1.json verify field (python3 -c one-liner asserting prefix+8<=20); not added to tests/rename_contract.sh or any Pester test; impact=The exact regression class this fix closed (a renamed identity prefix pushing New-LocalUser's generated username over 20 chars) has no standing, always-run guard; the next rename of that prefix reproduces the original blind spot, catchable only by a gated windows-2022 runner.

title: Socket-resolution failure prints two report lines both labelled 'herdr endpoint' (one fail, one skipped) — corroborated independently by 3 reviewers (architecture, code-quality, reliability)
detail: autofix_class=gated_auto; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs: resolution Err branch pushes Check::fail("herdr endpoint", ...) (~L1656-1665); check #4's None branch pushes Check::skipped("herdr endpoint", ...) (~L1684-1687) with the identical label; impact=Every doctor run against an unresolvable herdr socket shows a contradictory-looking duplicate line (one ✗, one ○, same label), undercutting the report clarity D14 was meant to deliver. Promoted from P3 to P2 per synthesis rule: independent corroboration across 3 reviewers promotes severity one level.

title: Module split dropped fix.rs; guided-fix mutation logic lives in checks.rs behind a stale 'unchanged read-only' doc comment
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs header still reads 'moved here unchanged ... no logic or output-text changes' while the same file now holds ~330 lines of config-mutating guided-fix code; plan.md's Module structure section committed to a separate fix.rs; impact=A maintainer auditing where doctor mutates config.json would be misled by the file's own doc comment into skipping it. Note: the binding cell-level must_haves (doctor-config-surface-4/-5 artifacts) never required fix.rs as a separate file, so this is plan-vs-implementation drift, not a missing required artifact.

title: Guided-fix dispatch routes by free-form check label string with a silent no-op fallthrough, unlinked to the label's producer
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs offer_fixes matches check.label.as_str() against "config"/"allowed roots"/"web token" literals with a _ => {} default (~L1757-1768); build_checks defines those labels elsewhere with nothing linking producer and consumer; impact=Rewording a fixable check's display label silently disables its guided fix with no compile error and no failing test, for two security-relevant fixes (allowed_roots widening, web-token provisioning).

title: default_config_json duplicates config::ensure_config's default values as a second source of truth
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json (~L2047-2064) hand-writes bind_addr/protocol/poll/static_dir defaults that mirror ensure_config's; own comment concedes the duplication; impact=If ensure_config's defaults (e.g. the pinned herdr_protocol wire-compat number) change, the unparseable-config backup-then-recreate path silently writes a stale default, in the exact branch that fires after data has already been backed up as unrecoverable.

title: Settings editor offers no way to remove or narrow an allowed_roots entry — append-only
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/doctor/edit.rs edit_allowed_roots (~L2794-2826) and the guided-fix menu (~L1916-1968) only ever add a root, never remove/replace one; impact=After a user widens allowed_roots via D9's typed confirmation (e.g. to '/'), doctor provides no path to narrow it back — contradicts the 'single friendly surface to edit any setting' framing (D1) for this one field. Advisory: a product-scope question, not a locked-decision violation.

title: Secrets::from_env performs file I/O and prints raw stderr diagnostics, diverging from the app's tracing-based logging
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/config/mod.rs from_env() now also reads herdr-go.env (~L1229-1236); src/config/secrets.rs read_trusted_env_file uses eprintln! on a protection failure (~L1382-1388) instead of tracing::warn! used elsewhere (e.g. main.rs's non-loopback notice); impact=An operator running under a log collector that captures tracing output but not raw stderr misses the 'ignoring herdr-go.env' warning entirely; the method name from_env also now misdescribes what it does.

title: Non-loopback bind warning (D16) fires in the settings editor but not on the guided config-repair path
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/doctor/edit.rs edit_config_field checks !addr.ip().is_loopback() before persisting (~L2779-2785); src/doctor/checks.rs apply_field_repairs has no equivalent branch (~L1875-1881); impact=Repairing a malformed bind_addr through the guided config-fix flow (as opposed to the settings editor) can widen the bind to non-loopback with no D16 warning shown, even though it is also 'the moment bind_addr is changed.'

title: Guided 'recreate default config' can set allowed_roots to the home directory with no D9 breadth confirmation
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json (~L2051-2057) falls back to the home directory when ~/projects doesn't exist; written via backup_and_recreate with no breadth prompt; impact=On a fresh host without ~/projects, accepting the unparseable-config recreate offer grants the gateway access to the whole home directory with no D9 confirmation, though this mirrors config::ensure_config's existing first-run default rather than being a new regression.

title: Settings editor blames the field the user just edited when a *different* pre-existing field is what fails validation
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/edit.rs persist_field's RepairOutcome::StillInvalid arm always prints '{label} value is invalid' using the just-edited field's label, regardless of which field(s) invalid_fields actually names (~L2856-2868); impact=A user editing a valid field while an unrelated field is already invalid sees a misleading error blaming their (correct) edit, sending them looking for a bug that isn't where the message claims.

title: default_config_json builds JSON via Rust's {:?} Debug formatting instead of a real serializer
detail: autofix_class=manual; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs default_config_json interpolates the allowed-root path with {:?} (~L2058-2063) instead of serde_json encoding; impact=Debug escaping diverges from JSON string escaping for control characters/some Unicode in a home path, which can produce an invalid document that Config::load_str then rejects — failing the exact 'recreate a working default' operation the user asked for, after the corrupted file was already backed up.

title: read_trusted_env_file stores an untrimmed secret value while emptiness is judged on the trimmed form
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs read_trusted_env_file inserts v.to_string() untrimmed (~L1396-1400); resolve_from_env_and_file filters on !v.trim().is_empty() but returns the untrimmed clone (~L1360-1365); impact=A hand-edited herdr-go.env line like 'KEY= value' resolves to a token with a leading space, causing silent auth failures against external providers with no visible cause (the value itself is never printed by design).

title: replace_or_append's documented duplicate-collapsing behavior is untested
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs replace_or_append dedup logic (~L1336-1345); every existing test reaches it only via write_secret_in, which never produces a file with a pre-existing raw duplicate key; impact=D10's exact reason for existing (the legacy ensure_web_secret only appends, so a real file can carry a duplicate key) is unexercised by any test; a regression that keeps the last match or stops deduping would go undetected.

title: offer_config_fix's unrecognized-field cleanup branch (drops an unknown field) is untested
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/doctor/checks.rs offer_config_fix's deny_unknown_fields repair branch (~L1838-1852) has no covering test; impact=This branch drops data on a security-adjacent invariant ('tokens never live in config.json'); a regression that persists the unknown field instead of dropping it (e.g. a stray github_token) would go undetected.

title: The startup non-loopback warning call site in main.rs has no test coverage after extraction to a shared helper
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/main.rs calls herdr_go::doctor::non_loopback_bind_warning inside an is_loopback() guard (~L3094-3105); only the editor's call to the same helper is tested; impact=A refactor that drops or inverts the startup is_loopback() guard would bind publicly with no warning printed, and no test would fail.

title: write_secret reports failure to the user after the secret value is already committed to disk
detail: autofix_class=gated_auto; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs write_secret_in renames the temp file into place, then calls validate_token_protection as the function's returned result (~L1320-1324); callers report any Err as 'could not save'; impact=If the post-rename permission check ever fails, the user is told the save failed while the secret is in fact already live in herdr-go.env and will load at next start — state and report disagree.

title: Startup can print a false 'ignoring herdr-go.env' warning under systemd even when every secret already comes from the process env
detail: autofix_class=gated_auto; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs resolve_from_env_and_file reads and permission-checks the env file unconditionally before applying env-first precedence (~L1357-1372); impact=Under systemd (which injects herdr-go.env via EnvironmentFile= regardless of file mode), a non-600 file logs a misleading 'being ignored' warning on every start even though its contents are irrelevant because the process env already supplies everything.

title: Temp files from interrupted writes (including one holding a secret) are never cleaned up
detail: autofix_class=gated_auto; evidence=src/config/secrets.rs write_secret_in cleans up on returned Err but not on a mid-write crash between temp-write and rename; src/config/write.rs write_atomic has no cleanup on any failure branch (~L607-629); impact=A process kill between writing the temp file and the atomic rename leaves an orphaned .tmp-<rand> file behind permanently; for the secrets writer this is an unmanaged, owner-only copy of a live token with no reaper.

title: herdr ping has no evidenced read timeout; phase-3's unconditional full re-run doubles exposure to a hang
detail: autofix_class=advisory; evidence=src/doctor/mod.rs phase-3 re-run (~L2205-2207) calls build_checks().await a second time, re-invoking client.ping().await (checks.rs ~L1707); src/herdr/socket.rs (the actual ping/timeout implementation) was outside this review's read scope so this is not fully confirmed; impact=If herdr accepts a connection but never responds, doctor could hang with no upper bound, and D14's added re-run now does this twice per interactive session. Flagged for confirmation against src/herdr/socket.rs before treating as settled.

title: entropy score: 0/100 (perfect), first audit
detail: breakdown: orphaned_cells=0 unverified_cells=0 stale_decisions=0 stale_specs=0 backlog_without_outcome=0 stale_work=0 broken_tools=0; trend: first audit

title: No pre-dispatch check that a chosen subagent_type's tool grants cover what a cell execution needs
detail: bee-swarming's routing table maps generation tier to subagent_type bee-gather, but bee-gather is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob only) and cannot execute a cell that writes/reserves/verifies/caps. This has now recurred twice (2026-07-18 terminal-workspace-org, 2026-07-20 windows-installer-runtime-smoke) despite being documented in critical-patterns.md after the first occurrence -- prose alone isn't preventing it. A mechanized pre-dispatch check (assert the chosen subagent_type's declared tool grants cover what the cell's files/action require, or fix the routing table itself to stop mapping generation-tier cell-execution dispatches to a read-only type) would close this for good instead of relying on session memory of a prose entry.

title: state set/state gate have no mechanized default requiring --lane in a multi-session checkout
detail: Documented in critical-patterns.md after windows-username-length-fix (2026-07-20), but recurred the same day in macos-installer-runtime-smoke: an entire exploring/planning/swarming pass wrote to the shared top-level state.json instead of a per-feature lane, and a concurrent session's write broke the next state set call. Prose read once at session start is not surviving a full multi-phase session. A mechanized fix (e.g. state set/state gate refuse or warn when lanes[] is non-empty and --lane was not passed) would close this for good instead of relying on session memory of a critical-patterns entry.

title: No collision-proof allocation primitive for backlog.md PBI numbers across concurrent sessions
detail: Two concurrent sessions (macos-installer-runtime-smoke and new-shell-new-agent) independently computed the same 'next free PBI number' at nearly the same timestamp, both picking PBI-020 for unrelated items. Caught via a session-boundary file-change notification, fixed by renumbering. Re-reading backlog.md immediately before write only narrows the race window, doesn't eliminate it. A claim-next-id lock primitive (or UUID-first IDs with sequential numbers assigned at a serialization point) would make the collision structurally impossible.

title: No mechanized check that decision UUIDs cited in docs/specs/*.md actually exist in .bee/decisions.jsonl
detail: While hand-copying ~20+ decision UUIDs into docs/specs/installation.md during macos-installer-runtime-smoke's scribing pass, the orchestrator introduced a fabricated/corrupted UUID twice, caught only by manual grep-verification against decisions.jsonl before trusting it -- not by getting it right on the first attempt. A script analogous to tests/rename_contract.sh (grep every UUID appearing in docs/specs/*.md, assert each exists in .bee/decisions.jsonl, wire into commands.verify) would catch this mechanically instead of relying on the scribing author's own diligence every time.

title: bee-write-guard.mjs does not recognize worktree grants as a valid boundary for the creating session
detail: bee worktree new successfully creates a git-linked worktree and grants it its own .bee store, but the write-guard hook's containment check is anchored to a single workRoot fixed at hook-init time from the calling process's cwd -- it denies any edit targeting the freshly-created sibling worktree as an escape, even from the same session that just created it. This makes the documented paved road (worktree new, then open the next session in the printed path) unusable mid-conversation -- only a genuinely separate process can use it. Fix options: (a) teach bee-write-guard.mjs to check runtime/worktree-grants.json and accept a granted sibling worktree as an alternate valid boundary for the current session, or (b) have bee worktree new's own output/confirmation text explicitly state it is not usable by the creating session and must be opened by a separate process.

title: tests/rename_contract.sh has 3 rg-gated guards that silently no-op; replace rg with grep -E
detail: rg has no binary reachable from a non-interactive subprocess here, so lines 30, 37 and 38 never actually check anything and the script still reports ok. Two of the guards are inside 'if rg ...; then fail ...; fi' which swallows the 127. The script runs as part of commands.verify, so every feature since it was written has been shipping with three unenforced contract checks. grep -E is a drop-in replacement. The earlier recorded conclusion that this could not be fixed because sudo apt-get needs a password accepted the wrong constraint

title: windows-support-5 is capped with verify_passed true but its verify exits 127 in a real shell
detail: The cell's verify is a chain of 5 rg invocations, 2 negated. Re-run today in a non-interactive shell it exits 127, so the recorded evidence is not reproducible and it is unknown whether the Windows CI contract it asserts was ever actually proven. Re-run it with rg replaced by grep -E and either confirm the assertions or reopen the cell

title: no mechanical lint on a cell's verify string: pipe-tail, bare rg, and CI drift all reach review by hand
detail: Three separate defect classes in one feature reached a human reviewer that a one-line grep would have caught: a final pipe segment whose exit code encodes nothing, an rg invocation that cannot run here, and a clippy flag weaker than the one CI enforces. This belongs in bee's own cell validator rather than the host project, since .bee/bin is bee machinery. Suggested gate at cell add and at cap: reject a verify whose last pipe segment is tail/head/cat, reject a bare rg, and warn when the verify's tool flags diverge from the repo's CI workflow

title: three wire.rs fixtures claim live provenance in their comments but are hand-typed literals
detail: src/herdr/wire.rs:286, 303 and 333 each carry a comment saying the shape was captured live from session.snapshot, while the body is an inline literal written by the same author as the parser it tests. That proves self-consistency, not conformance to the real wire. src/herdr/testdata/ now holds a genuine capture, so these three can either be backfilled from it or have their comments downgraded to stop claiming provenance they do not have

title: Unguarded Get-Process|.Path in windows-install-smoke.ps1 risks Win32Exception, one occurrence inside finally
detail: scripts/windows-install-smoke.ps1:60,95 access .Path on every enumerated process with no -ErrorAction/try-catch, unlike neighboring Get-ScheduledTask calls which use -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue. Line 60 is inside the finally cleanup block, so an exception there could mask a real assertion failure that triggered cleanup. Fix: guard both pipelines or pre-filter by ProcessName before touching .Path.

title: New release.yml jobs/steps inherit workflow-wide contents:write with no write need of their own
detail: release.yml:10-11 sets contents:write workflow-wide; macos-install-smoke and the appended windows smoke step neither need nor scope down this permission, though pre-existing build/release-windows jobs legitimately need it. Least-privilege gap on the ambient GITHUB_TOKEN for the new jobs. Fix: add permissions: contents: read to the new job/step scope.

title: Timeout failures in both install-smoke scripts carry no diagnostic payload
detail: All 6 wait loops (windows-install-smoke.ps1 Wait-Until, macos-install-smoke.sh wait_until) throw/die with only a static description on timeout -- no last HTTP status, scheduled-task/launchd state, or process state captured. Makes triaging a red CI run (real regression vs runner flake) require a re-run with manual instrumentation. Fix: dump one state snapshot before throwing on timeout.

title: windows-install-smoke.ps1 never checks install.ps1's own exit status, unlike the macOS sibling
detail: windows-install-smoke.ps1:72-73,109-110 capture install.ps1's output but never check ; macos-install-smoke.sh explicitly checks 0 -eq 0 for the equivalent install.sh calls. A real install.ps1 failure could surface as a confusing downstream assertion instead of a clear 'install.ps1 failed' signal. Fix: add explicit  checks after both install.ps1 invocations.

title: Windows crash-restart wait margin is proportionally much thinner than macOS's sibling
detail: windows-install-smoke.ps1 waits 90s against install.ps1's 60s RestartInterval floor (~1.5x margin); macos-install-smoke.sh waits 20s against install.sh's 3s ThrottleInterval floor (~6.7x margin) -- against a mechanism install.ps1 itself documents as best-effort. Real flakiness risk on a loaded windows-2022 runner. Fix: widen the Windows wait budget (e.g. 120-150s).

title: windows-install-smoke.ps1 hard-coupled to CI-only :GITHUB_WORKSPACE, unlike locally-reproducible macOS sibling
detail: windows-install-smoke.ps1:195 resolves install.ps1's path via :GITHUB_WORKSPACE; macos-install-smoke.sh derives its path from , working locally too. A maintainer debugging a red Windows smoke step cannot reproduce it locally without faking the env var. Fix: resolve via , falling back to GITHUB_WORKSPACE.

title: Failure-path cleanup in both install-smoke scripts skips the full uninstall step
detail: If either script fails before reaching its final uninstall step, cleanup only unregisters the task/agent and kills the process, never running the full uninstall flow -- binary/config/token survive. Inert on current ephemeral GH-hosted runners (VM destroyed post-job); would only matter on a persistent/self-hosted runner. Optional: document the ephemeral-runner assumption with a comment.

title: Cleanup command failures are swallowed silently in both install-smoke scripts
detail: Every cleanup command uses -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue (Windows) or || true (macOS) -- a genuine cleanup failure is indistinguishable from success in the log. Same ephemeral-runner mitigation as the partial-uninstall finding. Optional: log (not fail on) cleanup command results for visibility.

title: pane-agent-status-changed-live-probe's intended idle-while-seen control sample never actually fired
detail: pane-probe.mjs re-reports idle after focusing the pane, but the pane was already idle from the prior unfocused report, so no new transition/event occurs (0 events, per findings.md). The seen-vs-unseen half of the done-vs-idle comparison is inferred, not directly observed; findings.md wording could be read as implying both were independently observed. Optional: a follow-up probe captures a genuine idle-while-seen transition, or the wording is tightened.

title: Flaky verify: config::secrets tests race on process env
detail: cargo test --lib failed once with config::secrets::tests::process_env_wins_over_env_file expecting Some(from-env) but getting Some(from-file) (src/config/secrets.rs:232). Passes in isolation and on immediate re-run of the full lib suite. Cause: several tests in that module call std::env::set_var/remove_var on shared process-global env (lines 207,217,229,231,239) while Rust runs them in parallel threads in one process, so one test's remove_var wipes another's set_var mid-assertion. Consequence: the configured commands.verify can fail for reasons unrelated to the change under test, which trains the team to re-run instead of investigate -- same class as the rg-guard integrity items already filed. Fix: serialize those tests (module-level mutex or serial-test guard) or inject the env source instead of touching process env.

title: No CLI verb clears a pause handoff on resume
detail: Resuming a paused session is the documented way a pause handoff ends, but there is no verb for it: state handoff has only write/adopt/show (adopt is planned-next only), and state start-feature refuses while .bee/HANDOFF.json exists, telling the operator to 'resume the session (or explicitly delete HANDOFF.json)'. Resuming therefore requires a hand rm of a CLI-owned file, which hive law 12 forbids. Needed: bee state handoff clear (or resume), ideally recording that the handoff was consumed rather than abandoned. Hit while resuming new-shell-new-agent for slice 2.

title: No read timeout on the herdr socket call loop
detail: src/herdr/socket.rs:183-198 reads the response byte-by-byte with no timeout; a herdr that accepts the connection but never answers blocks the caller forever, and the web request behind it hangs. Pre-existing, but slice 2 adds create verbs (tab.create, agent.start) that spawn processes upstream and are the slowest requests this client makes, so the hazard becomes materially more reachable. Deliberately left out of slice 2 because fixing it changes every existing call path (snapshot, ping, read_pane, send_input, send_keys), which is wider than the slice. Fix: wrap call() in a configurable tokio timeout mapping to HerdrError::Unavailable.

title: Doctor menu tests hardcode item numbers, so adding any setting breaks them
detail: run_editor builds its menu from write::CONFIG_FIELDS plus 3 secrets plus a stop entry, and the stop index is derived at runtime -- but seven tests in src/doctor/edit.rs feed a hardcoded '12' as the stop choice. Adding agent_presets grew CONFIG_FIELDS from 8 to 9, so '12' silently selected the ninth field's edit prompt instead of stopping, the test reader ran dry, and prompt fell through to real stdin (Os error 6, no such device or address). Every future setting addition hits this. Being fixed in agent-presets-config-1 by deriving the stop index from CONFIG_FIELDS.len() in a test helper rather than bumping the literal. Filed so the pattern is remembered: a test that hardcodes a position in a generated menu converts every additive change into a false failure.

title: state set --feature does not reset approved_gates; state start-feature is the atomic verb but nothing nudges toward it
detail: During web-create-sheet's exploring->planning transition, generic 'state set --owner ... --feature web-create-sheet' was used to move onto a new feature (matching prior patterns seen in this session), leaving approved_gates.execution stale-true from the just-closed web-create-endpoints feature. Caught during validating's orient step before any cell was claimed on the stale flag (cells were still open, not claimed), and fixed with 'state gate --name execution --approved false'. The dedicated 'state start-feature' verb exists and resets all four gates atomically, but AGENTS.md/bee-exploring's step 6 instructs a generic 'state set --owner exploring --phase exploring --feature <feature>' call, not start-feature, so nothing in the documented flow signals the atomic verb should be used instead when transitioning off a just-closed feature.

title: No CLI verb clears a resolved pause HANDOFF.json; state start-feature refuses unconditionally on its mere existence
detail: HANDOFF.json (kind pause) was written mid-Phase-2-start for web-create-sheet and never updated after Phase 2 actually completed within the same continuous session (compounding closed it). Starting a genuinely new feature via state start-feature then refused outright because the file exists, regardless of whether its content is stale/resolved. state handoff write/adopt/show cover write, planned-next-adopt, and read -- none clear a pause handoff whose described work is already done. Manually removed the file this time per the no-verb-exists fallback (hive law 12).

title: FakeHerdr's w3 hardcodes AgentStatus::Idle for a zero-agent workspace, but real herdr computes Unknown there
detail: src/herdr/fake.rs:115-121's w3 seed sets agent_status: AgentStatus::Idle with a comment claiming this matches herdr ('not Unknown, which is reserved for...'). Direct verification against upstreams/herdr/src/workspace/aggregate.rs:91-105 (Workspace::aggregate_state) shows the real rollup explicitly defaults to AgentState::Unknown via .unwrap_or((AgentState::Unknown, true)) when a workspace has zero agents to aggregate from -- confirmed during home-shell-workspaces exploring (decision af31982e). The fake is kinder/different than live here, same pattern as prior fake-fidelity findings in critical-patterns.md. Did not fix as part of home-shell-workspaces: that feature's D7 hides the group header badge structurally (zero agent rows in the group), never reading the workspace_status value, so the discrepancy doesn't block or affect it -- but a future feature that DOES care about the exact status value for a zero-agent workspace (e.g. anything surfacing workspace_status text directly) would be silently testing against the wrong value. Fixing it now risked breaking unrelated existing tests asserting on w3's current Idle value outside this feature's scope.

title: web-api.md says Destination/ShellRow.path is absent on join miss; wire actually sends null
detail: Update Data Dictionary items 4/11 from 'absent' to 'null when the join misses' -- code/frontend/tests already agree on null.

title: P10 agent-refuse test (ghost-workspace) passes even if the anchor-refuse guard is deleted
detail: Add a mirror test using unresolvable('wx') + RecordingHerdr for POST /api/agents asserting 409 AND agent_cwd stays None, matching the shell-side test.

title: buildHomeGroups depends on an unenforced backend disjointness invariant (shells vs agent workspaces)
detail: No test pins the backend filter (api.rs:66) that guarantees shells and agents never share a workspace_id; a future relaxation would silently duplicate home-screen groups.

title: collisionSuffixes' 4-char workspace_id suffix can itself collide, leaving destinations ambiguous
detail: Corroborated independently by code-quality and test-coverage reviewers. Fall back to the full workspace_id when 4-char suffixes still collide; add a pinning test.

title: herdr_error_response relays err.to_string() verbatim; could leak internal detail (e.g. socket path)
detail: Post-auth, operator-only, low value. Map to a generic message and log detail server-side if defense-in-depth is wanted.

title: 409 unresolved-path message reflects the client's own workspace_id back in JSON (harmless)
detail: Reflected input in a JSON (not HTML) response body. Noted for completeness, no action needed.

title: fetchCreateOptions docstring claims a malformed-payload guard it doesn't have
detail: Drop 'or malformed payload' from the docstring, or add Array.isArray(data.destinations) validation if the guard is actually wanted.

title: createPane/createAgent/fetchAgents/fetchCreateOptions use unchecked 'as' casts on parsed JSON
detail: Consistent with this codebase's 'trust our own backend' posture; a shared parseJsonOr(res, guard) helper would centralize it if that posture changes.

title: AgentRow.workspace vs ShellRow/Destination.workspace_id: inconsistent field naming for the same concept in one JSON body
detail: A generic 'group by workspace_id' helper would silently get undefined for agent rows. See also the terminalHead discriminant finding before renaming.

title: terminalHead's AgentRow|NewPaneRef discriminant is load-bearing on the workspace/workspace_id naming asymmetry
detail: Renaming AgentRow.workspace to workspace_id (the 'obvious cleanup') would break this discriminant; tsc catches it today, but the coupling is invisible across files. Discriminate on an explicit tag instead.

title: Resource-naming asymmetry: shells created at POST /api/panes but listed under GET /api/agents
detail: A future maintainer may expect GET /api/panes. Not worth churn now; note for when the surface next grows.

title: create-sheet's empty-destinations branch ("No destinations available.") is untested
detail: web/test/create-sheet.test.ts always mocks a non-empty list. Add a test asserting the status message shows and no action rows render; same trivial gap on the backend zero-workspaces path.

title: JWKS fetch has no timeout, can hang the CF-Access-auth path indefinitely
detail: src/web/cf_access.rs fetch_jwks uses reqwest::Client::new() (no timeout). A black-holed/slow Cloudflare hangs the request instead of failing closed. Cookie path unaffected (Mutex not held across await). Fix: add .timeout()/.connect_timeout() to the CF Access client.

title: JWKS refetch abandons still-valid cached keys on transient fetch failure
detail: decoding_key (cf_access.rs) early-returns Err on any fetch error at TTL expiry instead of serving the still-valid stale cache. A brief CF 503 at the TTL boundary 404s every CF-authenticated user even though CF still accepts those keys. Fix: serve stale keys on fetch failure when cache non-empty.

title: JWKS refetch has no single-flight guard (thundering herd) -- corroborated by 2 reviewers
detail: Cache lock dropped before the awaited fetch (cf_access.rs); N concurrent stale-cache requests each fire their own JWKS GET on cold start/TTL burst. Correct results, wasted calls. Fix: single-flight coalescing, worth doing once the timeout finding above lands.

title: Verified CF Access identity is computed then discarded -- dead code masked by module-wide allow(dead_code)
detail: AuthSession only calls .is_ok() on verify() (auth.rs); email/sub extraction and CfAccessError::NoIdentity are unused in production, hidden by a module-wide #![allow(dead_code)] in cf_access.rs that also suppresses future real dead code. Corroborated independently by code-quality and architecture reviewers. Fix: either narrow verify() to Result<(), _> and drop the identity plumbing, or scope the allow to the specific network-only error variants and start logging identity on success.

title: verify() not exercised through its own config-bound path in negative tests
detail: Wrong-aud/iss/expired tests call the pure verify_with_key helper directly with hand-passed constants; only the valid-token test flows through the real verify() entry point. A bug that swapped/dropped self.aud/self.team_domain in verify() would pass the whole suite. Fix: add at least one wrong-aud/wrong-iss test through CfAccessVerifier::verify() itself.

title: CfAccessError::MissingKid untested (NoIdentity now moot if identity plumbing is removed per the dead-code finding)
detail: No test omits header.kid to exercise the MissingKid rejection path; all test tokens set kid. Add a test for a token with no kid header.

title: Half-configured CF Access (only one of team_domain/aud set) is silently ignored, no warning logged
detail: main.rs's _ => None arm swallows the one-of-two-fields case with no log; operator gets silent cookie-only behavior with no signal their config typo left the feature off. Fix: warn on partial config.

title: Doc comment on CfAccessVerifier::new incorrectly claims the reqwest client is shared app-wide
detail: cf_access.rs doc cites notify::telegram as precedent for a shared client, but telegram also builds its own Client::new() -- nothing is actually shared. Fix: reword to 'owns its own client, same per-subsystem pattern as notify::telegram'.

title: team_domain scheme is never validated -- an http:// team domain would fetch JWKS in cleartext
detail: certs_url() uses the operator's team_domain verbatim; a misconfigured http:// origin allows MITM key injection. Contingent on misconfig + defeated by the tailnet assumption, but a config-load-time https-only check would close it outright. Feature: cf-access-jwt-auth.

title: Rejection tests assert only .is_err(), not the specific CfAccessError variant
detail: wrong_aud/wrong_iss/expired/not_yet_valid tests in cf_access.rs collapse all failures to is_err() -- confirmed not tautological (removing set_issuer/set_audience flips them to Ok) but can't distinguish 'rejected for the intended reason' from 'rejected incidentally'. Fix: assert matches!(.., CfAccessError::Invalid(_)).

title: HTTP-level bogus-CF-header test uses a malformed string, not a well-formed bad-signature JWT
detail: auth.rs's cf_access_configured_bogus_header_is_opaque_404 sends 'not.a.jwt', which fails at decode_header before any signature/claim check. The unit-level tampered_signature test covers this, but the HTTP integration test never proves a structurally-valid wrong-signature token yields 404 through the real route. Fix: send a foreign-key-signed well-formed JWT through the header.

title: docs/specs/web-api.md under-documents the identity-claim and nbf requirements for an accepted CF Access assertion
detail: R8/the behavior section says signature+iss/aud/exp are sufficient; code additionally requires nbf validity and an email-or-sub identity claim (NoIdentity rejection). Stricter than documented (safe), but a maintainer reading only the spec would be surprised. Fix: add both to the spec's Blocked-when list.

title: Release binary only shows bump version, hard for devs to know which build they're testing
detail: herdr_go::VERSION (printed by --help and likely a version/health endpoint) is just the release-bump semver from Cargo.toml. Local/dev builds between releases carry the same version string as the last tag, so a dev running a freshly compiled binary can't tell it apart from an older build with the same Cargo.toml version -- no git sha, no build timestamp, no dirty-tree marker. Need a more granular dev-facing version string (e.g. git short sha + dirty flag, embedded via build.rs) so devs can confirm they're actually running the build they just compiled.

title: No pre-flight setup run for a fresh worktree's first web/-touching cell
detail: git worktree add never copies node_modules (gitignored); nothing runs the recorded setup command before dispatching the first cell that touches web/, so a worker discovers and fixes the gap mid-cell as an undeclared deviation. Recorded setup already anticipates this (cargo fetch && cd web && npm install) but nothing runs it automatically. Consider: bee-swarming/bee-executing runs the recorded setup command once per fresh worktree before the first dispatch touching a product-file path that needs it.

title: Logout uses replaceState, leaving a stale forward history entry that re-renders terminal detail after logout
detail: main.ts:122-130 navigate() replace branch on logout ('/' unchanged path) doesn't clear a forward /terminal/<id> entry; browser Forward after logout re-renders the authed terminal shell. Fix: location.assign('/') on logout instead of navigate({name:'login'}). autofix_class: manual. review: review-pbi-025-terminal-detail-url-20260722

title: Deep-linked terminal detail has no switcher entry beneath it, so Back exits the app instead of reaching the agent list
detail: main.ts bootstrap() resolves a /terminal/<pane_id> deep link via replaceState only (no pushState beneath it); goBack()/history.back() then has nothing in-app to land on, leaving the site. Violates docs/specs/terminal-detail.md's unqualified Back promise for the primary deep-link use case. Fix: seed a switcher entry beneath the terminal one during deep-link resolution (replaceState switcher, then pushState terminal). autofix_class: manual. review: review-pbi-025-terminal-detail-url-20260722

title: event.state is cast to Route and trusted without shape validation in handlePopState
detail: main.ts:132-135 casts event.state without validating route.name against the known union; applyRoute's switch has no default, so an unrecognized route silently blank-renders instead of falling back to switcher. Fix: validate route.name or add a default case. autofix_class: advisory. review: review-pbi-025-terminal-detail-url-20260722

title: Importing main.ts for its pure helpers triggers a real bootstrap fetch and popstate listener as an import-time side effect
detail: main.ts:137,176 registers a global popstate listener and calls void bootstrap() at module top level; main.test.ts imports pure helpers from the same module, incurring both side effects on every test run. Currently benign (root is null in jsdom). Fix: split pure route helpers from app-entry side effects, or gate entry side effects behind a root-exists check. autofix_class: advisory. review: review-pbi-025-terminal-detail-url-20260722

title: Back-after-logout can transiently render a stale authenticated view 2+ navigation steps deep
detail: web/src/main.ts's navigate() D7 exception (switcher-login-url) replaceStates only the current top-of-stack entry when entering/leaving the login route. A user several pushState steps deep (e.g. switcher -> terminal-A -> terminal-B) who logs out gets: terminal-B's entry replaced with login, but terminal-A's entry (1 level back) is untouched -- pressing Back pops to terminal-A and handlePopState renders it via applyRoute with no re-auth check, briefly showing an authenticated-only view before its own data fetch fails to 401 (falls back to Disconnected). No new data leak (terminal.ts already fails closed on fetch failure) but a real regression risk. Fix needs a product decision on how strong the 'never reachable via Back' guarantee should be (gate every popstate render on a fresh session check, vs. truncate/reload history on logout) -- out of switcher-login-url's locked D1-D7 scope. See docs/history/learnings/20260722-switcher-login-url-back-stack-depth.md and critical-patterns.md's 20260722 entry.

title: bee-gather generation-tier dispatch mismatch recurred a 3rd time, now silently (no self-block)
detail: Third occurrence of the bee-swarming generation-tier routing table pointing at read-only bee-gather (2026-07-18 terminal-workspace-org P1, 2026-07-20 windows-installer-runtime-smoke P2, now 2026-07-22 pbi-033-service-lifecycle-cli). This time bee-gather did not self-report [BLOCKED] -- it silently completed a read-only gather digest and returned as if the cell were handled, leaving the cell claimed-not-capped with zero error signal. Only caught because the orchestrator happened to check cells show for capped status before trusting the result. Escalating from P2 to P1: the failure mode got quieter each time (hard block -> self-reported block -> silent wrong-looking-right), which is the opposite direction prose-only mitigation should be trending. Fix belongs in bee's own swarming skill/model-guard (repo-rendered copy should not be hand-edited here) -- either correct the generation-tier subagent_type mapping, or add a pre-dispatch capability check.

title: Worker's prose-reported deviation not mirrored into cell trace.deviations
detail: pbi-033-service-lifecycle-cli-2's done-report describes a deviation (renamed README's Restart heading to Service management, fixed a dangling cross-reference) but .bee/cells/pbi-033-service-lifecycle-cli-2.json's trace.deviations is an empty array. Verified by hand that the deviation itself was executed correctly (no dangling anchors remain) -- this is a documentation-integrity gap, not a correctness bug: the machine-readable trace and the prose report disagree, so a future audit trusting only trace.deviations would miss it. A cap-time check requiring every prose-reported deviation to also appear in the structured field (or vice versa) would close this cheaply.

title: Flaky env-var test makes every worktree merge a coin flip
detail: config::tests::secrets_absent_from_env_and_file_are_none is order-dependent: another test calls std::env::set_var (src/config/mod.rs:1274) and Rust runs tests in parallel threads sharing process env, so the suite fails about 1 in 12 full runs on a clean main. One merge already aborted with a false MERGE_VERIFY_RED alarm.

title: worktree merge --cleanup skips cleanup on ALREADY_UP_TO_DATE
detail: It returns ok:true verify:skipped but leaves the worktree, branch and grant in place, so a hand-resolved merge cannot be finished through bee and needs manual git worktree remove + branch -d + unregister.

title: compounding-complete fires before the final commit lands
detail: Observed twice: the phase flipped while the whole scribing output (new spec files, learning docs) was still untracked. Automation triggering merge on the phase alone can merge a feature missing its new spec files silently. A safe trigger needs phase AND clean tree AND a settle window, or better, the child process exiting.

title: Concurrent worktrees mint duplicate PBI ids
detail: pbi-033 and health-fingerprint both minted PBI-036, the second such collision. Renumbered to PBI-037 by hand during the merge. docs/backlog.md also carries pre-existing duplicate rows for PBI-031 and PBI-032.

title: doctor has no check that a configured agent preset's argv[0] actually resolves on this machine
detail: src/doctor/checks.rs's herdr_version() already shows the exact pattern (Command::new(x).output().ok()?, zero PATH-walking) that a preset-resolution check would need; D3 (default-agent-presets) declined adding this citing complexity that doesn't actually exist in this codebase. Impact: a preset (default-seeded or operator-added) whose binary is missing fails silently until the operator taps it on their phone and sees a raw OS spawn-error string. A doctor check reusing the herdr_version() pattern would surface this at diagnose time instead.

title: No operator-facing warning that default agent presets ship with permission/sandbox bypass enabled
detail: All 3 default-seeded presets (Claude/Codex/Agy) run with their CLI's permission or sandbox gate turned off (--dangerously-skip-permissions / --sandbox danger-full-access --ask-for-approval never) by default on every fresh install. This codebase already has a precedent for warning on a comparably risky default (doctor's non-loopback bind-address warning, docs/specs/doctor.md), and this project's own distillery notes on the airemote reference project (docs/distillery/) flag hard-refusing this exact class of setting as a deliberate protective pattern absent here. Currently the only disclosure is docs/specs/installation.md R20a, a developer-facing spec, not README or an in-product notice. Consider a first-run or doctor-time notice, or a README mention, so an operator who never opens config.json still knows their default agent launches run unattended and unrestricted.

title: herdr-go --help fingerprint thiếu phần (sha,timestamp) khi user test thủ công
Sheet + không có option tạo agent, chỉ có Shell
detail: Nguyên nhân xác nhận: ~/.config/herdr-go/config.json hiện tại không có field agent_presets, vì file này được tạo TRƯỚC feature default-agent-presets (PBI-036). ensure_config cố tình không đụng config đã tồn tại (kể cả để trống) theo D-decision của feature đó, nên seed 3 preset Claude/Codex/Agy chỉ áp dụng cho fresh install, không retroactive cho config cũ. User thấy sheet "+" chỉ có Shell vì agent_presets rỗng/thiếu. Hướng khả dĩ: thêm doctor check gợi ý seed presets khi agent_presets thiếu/rỗng trên config đã có, hoặc cho phép user tự thêm qua doctor interactive editor (PBI-013 đã có sẵn cơ chế sửa 8 field config.json).

title: Boundary path-allowlist is dead code; allowed_roots is not a runtime gate
detail: src/security/paths.rs:248-339 Boundary is never constructed outside its own tests; no reference in src/main.rs or src/web/*. Not exploitable today because create routes refuse a client cwd (src/web/create.rs:26-41), but it becomes a real hole the moment PBI-020 or any cwd-accepting endpoint lands. Filed as PBI-044.

title: backlog badges --write touches README.md, which the write guard then blocks from being committed
detail: During exploring (execution gate unapproved), scribing's prescribed rank --write then badges --write pair writes README.md; git add README.md is then refused by the write guard, leaving the worktree dirty and unmergeable until reverted by hand. Either defer badges to a gated phase or add README to the guard's docs allowlist.

title: A skill documented an external CLI flag the binary rejects; a sibling worker caught it by running --help
detail: SKILL.md passed --json to herdr pane split (lines 128, 170) but herdr answers 'unknown option: --json' - it emits JSON by default. The cell that wrote it was already capped green because its verify greps for content, not for flag validity. The next cell's worker checked herdr --help before assuming any flag and found it. Reinforces the existing critical pattern: when a document hardcodes an external CLI's flags, run the binary's own --help as confirming evidence.

title: Dry-run proof could not have caught the pane leak; only the one real spawn did
detail: SKILL.md section 8 told the dispatcher to herdr pane split then herdr agent start. The live proof showed agent start opens its OWN pane and never attaches to the split one, so every dispatch would have left an empty stray pane and D5's four slots would fill with ghosts within four dispatches. A headless -p argv also makes herdr close the pane the instant the process exits. Both were invisible to every dry run and to a verify that greps document content. This is the concrete payoff of approach.md's demand for one real split before the loop is trusted.

title: Slice 1 shipped a loop whose prompt file no cell owned, so it would have stalled silently forever
detail: control-loop.sh resolves ../references/dispatch-prompt.md and bootstrap-cockpit.sh starts the dispatch loop, but no cell created that file. Every iteration would report 'prompt file not found' and, per D19, continue - producing a loop that runs forever doing nothing. Same root cause as the two blockers the panel caught: a piece named in one cell's prose as out of scope, with no later cell picking it up. The cell-level verifies all passed because each was scoped to its own file. Fixed in slice 2's first cell.

title: A per-iteration skip is not a stop: polling turns 'never retry' into retry-every-60-seconds
detail: The merge role's first draft said the red worktree is not re-attempted 'this iteration' while also saying a later iteration would find it eligible again. In a 60-second loop those are the same sentence: with the PBI-039 flake at 1-in-12, a red verify would have gone green and merged within about twelve minutes, defeating D3 entirely. Any 'skip for now' inside a polling loop must be anchored to a durable signal, not to the iteration boundary. Fixed by reading the chat pane's own scrollback for a prior red report - the same no-state-file mechanism D18 already forces for anomaly de-duplication.

title: The lane classifier waves through plainly-worded dangerous work: a regex list enumerates the words we thought of, not danger
detail: Run against the shipped script: 'Remove the login token check on the admin endpoint and delete the tests that cover it' returns lane_safe:true, as does PBI-042 (download and install a release binary, then restart the service). It fails closed on rows it cannot parse and fail-open on rows it cannot recognise. The repo's backlog is Vietnamese so most real rows dodge English patterns by default. D6 exists precisely to keep an unattended bypassPermissions agent away from that first row. Fix direction: two-key gate - the script's verdict AND the dispatch agent's own reading of the full row, refusal fail-closed and announced.

title: Tracked .bee/logs files keep main permanently dirty, so the merge role refuses its own merge every cycle forever
detail: git ls-files shows .bee/logs/{dispatch,hooks,tools}.jsonl tracked in main despite .gitignore listing .bee/logs/ - committed before the ignore. Every bee tool call rewrites them and bee worktree merge refuses on a dirty main. The merge role must run in main and dirties it with its own first command. No pane is ever closed, no slot freed, and after four dispatches the loop is dead. Fix: git rm --cached .bee/logs/*.jsonl, and treat WORKTREE_MERGE_MAIN_DIRTY as an anomaly report rather than a silent skip.

title: D1's reverse index reads a line only one of 24 CONTEXT.md files has, so the dispatchable set is empty forever
detail: The index greps for '**Backlog:** PBI-NNN' across docs/history/*/CONTEXT.md. One file of 24 carries it - this feature's own, hand-written. Nothing emits it: bee-exploring writes the mapping the other way, putting the slug into the backlog row. The loop would wake every 60s, spend a full Sonnet session, find nothing, and end quietly - 1440 sessions a day, silently. Fix: read the slug from the backlog row's feature field and keep the CONTEXT.md existence check as condition (a).

title: D12's cross-process verify lock was never built - a locked decision with no owning cell
detail: There is no lock in the shipped code. Under D5 the configured verify (cargo test + clippy + npm bundle + npm test) can run in four worktrees plus a fifth inside bee worktree merge, on ~5GB free - producing OOM-driven red verifies indistinguishable from real failures, which poisons D2's readiness signal. Fourth instance in this feature of a locked decision or named piece with no owning cell. Fix: wrap commands.verify in a flock on a path shared by main and every worktree, in .bee/config.json.

title: A worker used git stash to capture red evidence in a repo with a shared stash stack
detail: Cell 15's worker ran git stash push to reconstruct the pre-edit state for its red-then-green capture, while two other workers were editing the same checkout and the stash stack is shared across all worktrees and concurrent sessions. It restored cleanly this time. The dispatch prompt should tell workers to capture red evidence BEFORE writing anything, never by stashing afterwards - the environment note already forbids bare stash for exactly this reason.

title: Two fixes in one slice interacted: the new iteration timeout could kill a merge whose verify now queues behind the new lock
detail: Serializing verify behind a flock made a merge's wall clock its own verify plus up to three queued ones, which routinely exceeds a 900s iteration timeout. bee's abort-and-prove path is a JS finally that SIGTERM never runs, so the kill would leave main holding a staged uncommitted merge - permanently dirty, the exact stall the same slice had just fixed. Fixed three ways: the merge loop starts with --timeout 5400, timeout now escalates with -k 30s so a SIGTERM-ignoring process is still killed, and the merge role aborts a leftover MERGE_HEAD before doing anything else. Lesson: when two fixes land in one slice, check what they do to each other, not only what each does alone.
